title: DocsOps Task
description: A unit of work for a docs agent. Self-contained: an agent should be able to execute it with only the files referenced here plus the .docs-ops/ scaffolding.

type.description: Free-form category. Conventional values: frontmatter_audit, link_audit, parity_gap, sdk_signature_diff, snippet_validation, terminology_drift, section_reorder, parameter_fill, restructure.

owner_hint.description: Suggestion only; any agent may claim any task. Use 'claude' for tasks needing cross-file reasoning, 'copilot' for one-shot mechanical edits.

target_files.description: Repo-relative paths the task is allowed to read+modify. Tasks MUST NOT touch files outside this list (except .docs-ops/tasks/ and .docs-ops/evals/).

context_files.description: Repo-relative paths the agent should read but NOT modify. e.g. SDK source files, glossary, sibling platform pages.

instructions.description: Natural-language description of what to do. Should be specific enough that any reasonable agent produces the same result.

command.description: Shell command run from repo root. Exit 0 = pass.

expect_in_stdout.description: Optional: substring that must appear in stdout for pass.

rubric_must_not_regress.description: If true, post-change rubric score for each target_file must be >= pre-change score.

expected_outputs.description: Optional structured outputs the task should produce alongside file edits. e.g. a report JSON.

report_schema.description: Path to a JSON schema the report must validate against.

blocked_by.description: Task ids that must be in done/ before this task can be claimed.

created_by.description: Agent or human who created the task.
